# QZX - Quick Zap Exchange
> Verbose is Gold. Consistent cross-platform CLI diagnostics.

QZX is a cross-platform command-line tool designed to provide identical outputs and JSON formatting across Windows, macOS, and Linux.

## Philosophy: Verbose is Gold
Traditional Unix commands favor silence on success. QZX operates under the **"Verbose is Gold"** philosophy: every command returns a structured JSON payload with rich metadata and human-readable context. Responses always contain at least:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Descriptive status message",
  "details": { ... extra records ... }
}
```

---

## Installation & Usage

### CLI Installation
Install globally using npm/pnpm:
```bash
npm install -g qzx-cli
# or
pnpm add -g qzx-cli
```

### Basic Syntax
```bash
qzx <command> [arguments] [options]
```
Add `--json` anywhere in the command arguments to force raw JSON output (optimal for AI agents and automation scripts).

---

## Complete Command Index

### 📁 File Commands
*   **`changePermissions`** <path> <mode> [recursive] - Changes permissions of a file or directory
*   **`compressZip`** <zip_path> <source_path> [exclude_patterns] - Compresses a directory or file into a ZIP archive with custom exclusion rules
*   **`copyFile`** <source> <destination> [recursive] [force] - Copies a file or directory from source to destination
*   **`countLinesInFile`** <file_path> [recursive] [ignore_empty] - Counts the number of lines in files with support for wildcards and recursive searching
*   **`createDirectory`** <directory_paths> - Creates one or more directories at specified paths
*   **`decompressZip`** <zip_path> [target_path] - Extracts all files from a ZIP archive to a destination folder, safely and securely
*   **`deleteFile`** <target> [recursive] [force] - Deletes a file or directory from the filesystem
*   **`detectFileType`** <file_path> [detailed_info] - Identifies file type based on its magic number (file signature) rather than extension
    *Aliases: `wonderFileTypeMagic`*
*   **`downloadFile`** <url> <destination_path> [show_progress] [timeout] - Downloads a file from the Internet (similar to 'wget' or 'curl' in Unix)
*   **`findBrokenSymlinks`** [folder_path] [max_depth] - Scans directories for broken symbolic links (pointing to nonexistent files or folders)
*   **`findDuplicateFiles`** [scan_path] [min_size_kb] [max_depth] - Scans a directory for identical/duplicate files by file size matching and MD5 hashing
*   **`findFiles`** [search_path] [pattern] [recursive] [max_depth] [type] [size] [min_size] [max_size] [mtime] [newer_than] [older_than] [contains] [contains_regex] [case_sensitive] [exclude] [exclude_dir] [follow_symlinks] [sort_by] [reverse_sort] [format] [limit] - Advanced file search with multiple filtering options (like find + grep combined)
*   **`findLargeFiles`** <directory> <extension> <min_size> [recursive] [sort_by] - Searches for files with specific extensions that exceed a given size
*   **`findText`** <pattern> <target> [recursive] [regex] [case_sensitive] [file_pattern] [context_lines] [invert_match] [count_only] [max_matches] [colored] - Searches for text patterns in files with advanced filtering options
*   **`getFileHash`** <file_path> [algorithm] - Calculates cryptographic hashes (MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256) of a file
*   **`getHumanLanguageStats`** <file_path> [ignore_comments] [min_word_length] [languages] [recursive] [show_files_match] - Analyzes files to determine the percentage of text in different languages. Improved version using centralized file finder.
    *Aliases: `getHumanLanguageStatsFromFile`*
*   **`getProgrammingLanguageStats`** <file_path> [detailed] [languages] [recursive] - Analyzes files to determine the programming language and provides statistics about the code. Supports wildcards and recursive directory search.
    *Aliases: `getProgrammingLanguageStatsFromFile`*
*   **`inspectImage`** <image_path> - Inspects image dimensions, format, and size natively from headers (supports PNG, JPEG, GIF, BMP)
*   **`isDirectoryEmpty`** <directory_path> [include_hidden] - Checks if a directory is empty (contains no files or subdirectories)
    *Aliases: `wonderIfDirectoryEmpty`*
*   **`isFileBinary`** <file_path> [sample_size] [binary_threshold] - Analyzes a file to determine if it's binary or plain text
    *Aliases: `wonderIfFileIsBinary`*
*   **`isFileEmpty`** <file_path> [consider_whitespace] - Checks if a file is empty (has zero bytes)
    *Aliases: `wonderIfFileEmpty`*
*   **`listFiles`** [directory_path] [pattern] [recursive] - Lists files in a directory with support for wildcards and recursive searching
*   **`moveFile`** <source> <destination> [recursive] [force] - Moves or renames a file or directory from source to destination
*   **`readFile`** <file_path> [max_lines] - Reads and displays the content of a file
*   **`touchFile`** <path> [create_dirs] [content] - Creates an empty file or updates the timestamp of an existing file

### 🌐 Network Commands
*   **`checkDns`** <domain> - Queries A, AAAA, MX, TXT, NS, and CNAME DNS records for a given domain
*   **`checkSslCertificate`** <host> [port] - Inspects the SSL/TLS certificate of a hostname, checking validity dates and issuers
*   **`checkUrlStatus`** <url> [timeout] - Pings an HTTP URL, returning its status code, response time, and basic headers
*   **`getNetworkConfig`** [check_public] - Displays comprehensive network status (interfaces, local IPs, DNS, public IP, VPN detection)
*   **`testWebSpeed`** [max_seconds] - Measures internet download speed (Mbps) and HTTP ping latency (ms)

### 💻 System Commands
*   **`checkDependency`** <dependency> - Checks if a command-line dependency is installed in the system PATH and retrieves its version
*   **`checkSystemPath`** [binary_name] - Diagnoses the system PATH variable, lists broken or duplicate folders, and locates all instances of a binary
*   **`clearScreen`** - Clears the terminal screen
    *Aliases: `cls`, `clear`, `clrscr`*
*   **`commandsBridge`** <command> [args] - Safely executes system commands with detailed feedback
    *Aliases: `bridge`, `cmd`, `run`*
*   **`currentDir`** [full] - Shows the current working directory
    *Aliases: `pwd`, `cwd`, `dir`, `where`, `location`*
*   **`generateContent`** <file_path> [sample_size] [model] [custom_prompt] - Uses Gemini AI to analyze and explain file contents
    *Aliases: `explainfile`, `aianalyze`, `analyzeContent`*
*   **`getCPULoad`** [interval] - Gets information about current CPU usage
*   **`getCommandCount`** - Reports the total number of available commands in QZX
    *Aliases: `wonderCommandsAmount`*
*   **`getCurrentDate`** - Gets the current date in YYYY-MM-DD format
*   **`getCurrentTime`** - Gets the current time in HH-MM-SS-milliseconds format
*   **`getCurrentUser`** - Gets information about the currently logged in user
*   **`getDiskInfo`** [path] - Gets information about disk space usage
*   **`getDiskName`** [disk_path] - Gets disk name/model information for a disk or all disks
*   **`getEnvironmentInfo`** [detailed] - Displays detailed information about the system environment
    *Aliases: `environment`, `sysinfo`, `systeminfo`*
*   **`getGPULoad`** [detailed] - Displays information about GPUs in the system
    *Aliases: `gpuload`, `gpuinfo`, `gpustatus`*
*   **`getRAMInfo`** - Gets detailed information about system RAM usage
*   **`getSmartValues`** <disk> [format] - Retrieves disk health information using S.M.A.R.T. technology
*   **`getStartupPrograms`** - Lists all startup programs configured to run on system boot or user login
*   **`getToday`** [format] - Displays detailed information about the current date and time
    *Aliases: `today`, `now`, `datetime`, `fecha`*
*   **`inspectPort`** <port> [kill] - Checks if a port is in use, lists details of the process using it, and can optionally terminate it
*   **`isAdmin`** - Checks if the current user has administrative privileges
    *Aliases: `wonderIfAdmin`*
*   **`killProcess`** <pid> [force] - Terminates a process by its ID (similar to 'kill' in Unix)
*   **`listProcesses`** [filter_str] [sort_by] [limit] - Lists running processes (similar to 'ps' in Unix)
*   **`listSystemServices`** [status] - Lists system services and their running status (supports Windows and Linux)
*   **`runScript`** <script_path> [args] - Executes a script with parameters
*   **`shell`** [prompt] [history_file] [show_path] - Launches an interactive shell for QZX commands (alias for Terminal)
    *Aliases: `Terminal`, `Console`, `REPL`*
*   **`systemInfo`** - Gets information about the operating system and environment
*   **`term`** [prompt] [history_file] [show_path] - Launches an interactive QZX terminal (case-insensitive alias)
    *Aliases: `terminal`, `Terminal`, `TERMINAL`, `shell`, `Shell`, `SHELL`, `console`, `Console`, `CONSOLE`, `repl`, `REPL`, `Repl`, `term`, `TERM`*
*   **`terminal`** [prompt] [history_file] [show_path] - Launches an interactive terminal/shell for QZX commands
    *Aliases: `terminal`, `TERMINAL`, `Shell`, `shell`, `SHELL`, `Console`, `console`, `CONSOLE`, `REPL`, `repl`*
*   **`terminal`** [prompt] [history_file] [show_path] - Launches an interactive terminal/shell for QZX commands
    *Aliases: `terminal`, `TERMINAL`, `Shell`, `shell`, `SHELL`, `Console`, `console`, `CONSOLE`, `REPL`, `repl`*
*   **`terminal`** [prompt] [history_file] [show_path] - Launches an interactive terminal/shell for QZX commands
    *Aliases: `terminal`, `TERMINAL`, `Shell`, `shell`, `SHELL`, `Console`, `console`, `CONSOLE`, `REPL`, `repl`*
*   **`testDiskSpeed`** [test_path] [size_mb] - Benchmarks sequential write and read speeds (MB/s) of the filesystem at a target path
*   **`version`** - Displays the current version of QZX and system information
    *Aliases: `qzxVersion`*
*   **`welcome`** [full_info] - Displays the welcome screen with system information
    *Aliases: `welcome`, `hello`, `hi`*

### 🛠️ Development Commands
*   **`analyzeComplexity`** <file_path> [recursive] [format] - Analyzes code complexity metrics for files or projects
*   **`auditLanguages`** [scan_path] [threshold] - Audits a project workspace for programming languages and alerts on underrepresented ones in QZX
*   **`cleanDevCaches`** [scan_path] [dry_run] [max_depth] - Scans recursively for heavy development caches/dependencies (node_modules, __pycache__, dist) and purges them
*   **`compareFiles`** <file1> <file2> [mode] - Compara dos archivos y muestra sus diferencias, útil para IDE
*   **`createDocTemplatePython`** <file_path> [style] [overwrite] [preview] - Creates basic documentation templates for Python code that lacks docstrings
*   **`findDeadCode`** [scan_path] - Scans files for declared functions and classes, and identifies those with zero references in other files
*   **`getGitStatus`** [repo_path] - Provides a structured summary of the Git repository state (branch, remote, changes, recent commits)
*   **`getProjectTree`** [dir_path] [max_depth] [exclude_dirs] [include_files] - Generates a clean visual directory tree (ASCII and JSON) excluding heavy directories like node_modules
*   **`scaffoldC`** <project_name> [path] [with_tests] [build_system] - Creates a basic scaffolding for a C program
    *Aliases: `cScaff`, `newC`, `createC`, `makeScaffProgramC`*
*   **`scaffoldCpp`** <project_name> [path] [with_tests] [build_system] [cpp_standard] - Creates a basic scaffolding for a C++ program
    *Aliases: `cppScaff`, `newCpp`, `createCpp`, `makeScaffProgramCpp`*
*   **`scaffoldJavascript`** <project_name> [path] [with_tests] - Creates a basic scaffolding for a JavaScript/Node.js program
    *Aliases: `jsScaff`, `newJs`, `createJs`, `makeScaffProgramJavascript`*
*   **`scaffoldPhp`** <project_name> [path] [with_tests] - Creates a basic scaffolding for a PHP/Composer program
    *Aliases: `phpScaff`, `newPhp`, `createPhp`, `makeScaffProgramPhp`*
*   **`scaffoldPython`** <project_name> [path] [with_tests] [create_venv] - Creates a basic scaffolding for a Python program
    *Aliases: `pythonScaff`, `newPython`, `createPython`, `makeScaffProgramPython`*
*   **`scaffoldRust`** <project_name> [path] [binary] [with_tests] - Creates a basic scaffolding for a Rust program
    *Aliases: `rustScaff`, `newRust`, `createRust`, `makeScaffProgramRust`*
*   **`scaffoldTypescript`** <project_name> [path] [with_tests] - Creates a basic scaffolding for a TypeScript program
    *Aliases: `tsScaff`, `newTs`, `createTs`, `makeScaffProgramTypescript`*
*   **`scanProject`** [project_path] - Scans a project root to identify technologies, scripts, configuration files, and missing environment keys
*   **`traceCircularImports`** [scan_path] - Traces module imports recursively and identifies circular import dependencies (loops)
*   **`traceEnvVar`** <var_name> [project_path] [recursive] - Traces usage of an environment variable across code files, .env, and .env.example templates

---

For full details, interactive demos, and documentation, visit: https://qzx.yumbale.com
